---
title: "Exercises"
---

Follow these exercises to get familiar with the Northern Ireland (NI) spatial datasets and how to use them for transport analysis.

## Guided Exercises

1. [**01-loading-data.R**](https://github.com/Robinlovelace/pctni-hack/blob/main/exercises/01-loading-data.R): 
   Learn how to load Super Data Zones (SDZ) and Origin-Destination (OD) data, and join them to visualize commuting destinations.

2. [**02-flow-analysis.R**](https://github.com/Robinlovelace/pctni-hack/blob/main/exercises/02-flow-analysis.R): 
   Convert raw OD data into spatial flows (lines), calculate distances, and filter for commutable ranges.

3. [**03-advanced.R**](https://github.com/Robinlovelace/pctni-hack/blob/main/exercises/03-advanced.R): 
   A starting point for routing and accessibility analysis using the PCTNI route network.

## Working with the Scripts

We recommend opening these scripts in RStudio. You may need to install the following packages first:

```r
install.packages(c("sf", "tidyverse", "tmap", "stplanr", "od"))
```
